summary: Specifies the base16, base32, and base64 encodings, including the standard base32 alphabet (A-Z, 2-7) in section 6, the '=' padding rules mapping 5-byte input groups to 8-symbol output blocks, and handling of non-alphabet characters. Section 10 provides the canonical test vectors (e.g. BASE32("foobar") = "MZXW6YTBOI======") that conforming implementations must reproduce. Obsoletes RFC 3548.
title: The Base16, Base32, and Base64 Data Encodings
